applied_optimizations.name: Vectorized Triton kernel with BLOCK_SIZE=256
applied_optimizations.description: Changed from one-program-per-element to vectorized processing. Each program now processes BLOCK_SIZE=256 output elements in parallel using tl.arange and masked loads/stores. Reduced grid size from 35M to 138K programs, drastically reducing launch overhead. Uses int64 strides to avoid overflow.

attempted_but_failed.name: 1D grid with n_elements programs
attempted_but_failed.reason: Grid size > 10M causes silent failures on ROCm Triton

attempted_but_failed.name: Non-vectorized 3D grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correct but 24x slower than PyTorch due to launch overhead

top_bottlenecks: Memory bandwidth for loading kernel window

notes: ROCm Triton has grid size limitations - 1D grids > 10M elements fail silently
Must use PyTorch tensor strides (int64) and cast indices to tl.int64 to avoid overflow
Vectorized approach with BLOCK_SIZE=256 achieves 1.85x speedup over PyTorch
Key insight: process multiple output elements per program to amortize launch overhead
torch.cuda.synchronize() after kernel launch is critical to catch errors
